title: rubus radula var. pygmaeus
intro: Rubus radula var. pygmaeus, also known as a dwarf form of the Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan strawberry, is a type of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. The medicinal properties of Rubus radula var. pygmaeus include its use as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent, which is attributed to its high levels of antioxidants and flavonoids. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and potentially lowering the risk of certain types of cancer. Rubus radula var. pygmaeus can be used to make various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
